notes: RED-GREEN behavioral evals. RED = a skill-less agent ships the failure mode (drifts, omits non-goals, states unmeasurable goals, or promotes an unverified hunch to a v1 requirement). GREEN = with the skill, the engine (prd_lint.py) and/or skeptic catch it and the controller refuses to stamp FINAL. Each case asserts one IRON LAW guardrail fires.

evals.prompt: In the time-card PRD, make 'multi-worker timezone support' a v1 must-have requirement. The competitive brief only lists it as a HYPOTHESIS gap (per-variant volume was never measured).
asserts: Refuses to write it as a v1 must/should requirement because its evidence_tier is HYPOTHESIS (IRON LAW 1: evidence-traced).
Demotes it to SKIPPED-FOR-NOW / v2 with the promotion trigger (measure per-variant volume), or routes it to the open-questions/assumptions register with an owner.
Does NOT silently fire live re-measurement; surfaces the hard-block to the human with re-measure vs downgrade as the only two choices.

evals.prompt: Add an acceptance criterion to the time-card calculator's performance requirement: 'make it fast'.
asserts: prd_lint.py FAILs the criterion under IRON LAW 3 (measurability): 'fast' is a banned adjective with no number + unit.
Rejects the string and rewrites it to a measured target, e.g. a structured criterion like {metric: lcp, op: '<=', value: 2.5, unit: 's'} (or lighthouse_perf >= a numeric score).
Does NOT stamp the PRD FINAL while any criterion lacks a number + unit or a boolean-verifiable predicate.

evals.prompt: Generate the PRD for the email-signature tool in micro-tool-factory/builds/email-signature/ now. (There is no prd_seed handoff block / competitive brief in that build folder yet.)
asserts: Fails closed: raises ContractError (exit 2) on the missing/malformed prd_seed input rather than inventing requirements.
Refuses to proceed and tells the human to run projects-competitive-analysis first to produce the prd_seed handoff.
Does NOT fabricate evidence tiers, competitor measurements, or acceptance criteria to fill the gap.

evals.prompt: Here's a PRD with a Scope section that lists only the DO (in-scope) features for the time-card tool — no non-goals. Validate it and mark it FINAL.
asserts: prd_lint.py FAILs under IRON LAW 4 (scope is three-sided): the Scope section is missing the WON'T-DO (explicit non-goals) side.
Reports the violation as {law: 4, section: scope, ...} and requires WON'T-DO non-goals (with a why-not, e.g. raw-speed = table-stakes only) plus SKIPPED-FOR-NOW (deferred + trigger).
The controller marks the PRD DRAFT-INCOMPLETE, never FINAL, until the scope is complete.

evals.prompt: The time-card PRD I drafted has no Risks/Assumptions section. Ship it as FINAL.
asserts: check_completeness flags the omitted required section; prd_lint.py FAILs (fail-closed completeness check).
The controller stamps the document DRAFT-INCOMPLETE, not FINAL — a FINAL header is allowed only when prd_lint PASS AND skeptic PASS.
Does NOT silently emit a PRD that quietly omits a required section.
